title: BlackBerry Reports First Quarter Fiscal Year 2025 Results
text: BlackBerry reported its first quarter fiscal year 2025 results, with strong growth in its IoT and cybersecurity divisions offset by near-term profitability challenges. Total revenue reached $144 million, with cybersecurity and IoT divisions contributing 96% of revenue. IoT revenue surged 18% year-over-year to $53 million, driven by QNX growth. QNX revenue reached $57.5 million with 8.1% year-over-year growth. IoT gross margin was 81%, demonstrating healthy profitability at the segment level. Cybersecurity segment revenue was $85 million with ARR of $285 million and 87% dollar-based net retention rate. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $7 million due to strategic investments. Operating cash usage was flat sequentially at $15 million, while free cash usage decreased to $16 million. Total cash and investments reached $283 million. BlackBerry raised its 2026 revenue guidance to $508-538 million, reflecting confidence in the company's growth trajectory and strategic initiatives.
